evals.name: basic-search-uses-skill-search-cli
evals.prompt: Find me an installable agent skill for working with chezmoi dotfiles. I want to compare the top few candidates and pick one to install.
evals.expected_output: The agent invokes the skills-searcher skill, runs `skill-search search` (not raw gh / Sourcegraph queries), and presents a ranked candidate list with stars/installs/sources. It does not ask the user which backend to query.
expectations: Runs `skill-search search` (or `~/bin/skill-search search`, or the full `~/.agents/skills/skills-searcher/scripts/skill-search search` path) with a query containing 'chezmoi' or 'dotfiles'
Does NOT issue raw `gh api /search/code`, `gh skill search`, `src search`, or `npx skills find` invocations directly to assemble results
Does NOT ask the user to choose a backend (gh-skill vs Sourcegraph vs GitHub code vs npx-skills) — runs them in parallel via the CLI
Surfaces multiple candidate rows with at least one of: stars, installs, sources count, or repo path
If recommending a specific skill to install, reads the candidate's `SKILL.md` (via the preview/url column or a Read of the path) before recommending

evals.name: fresh-environment-runs-doctor-first
evals.prompt: I just set up a brand new machine and cloned my dotfiles. Find me a skill for reviewing pull requests on GitHub.
evals.expected_output: Because the environment is fresh, the agent runs `skill-search doctor` first to confirm backends are wired up, then runs the search. It does not assume gh / src / npx are all configured.
expectations: Runs `skill-search doctor` (or `--json doctor`) before — or alongside — the first search call
After doctor, runs `skill-search search` with a query referencing pull requests, code review, or PR review
If doctor reports a degraded backend, mentions which backend is unavailable rather than silently failing
Does not skip the search step on the basis of doctor output unless ALL backends are unavailable

evals.name: github-rate-limit-degrades-to-no-enrich
evals.prompt: I'm getting `gh: API rate limit exceeded for user ID` errors when I try to search for skills. I still need to find a skill for managing iOS simulators today.
evals.expected_output: The agent diagnoses the rate limit, runs the search with `--no-enrich` (and/or relies on Sourcegraph + npx-skills results), and tells the user how to check the reset window. It does not retry GitHub search in a tight loop.
expectations: Runs `skill-search search` with `--no-enrich` — OR explicitly relies on the Sourcegraph and npx-skills backends while GitHub is degraded
Mentions checking `gh api rate_limit` (or shows the reset time) so the user knows when GitHub recovers
Does NOT retry the same `gh skill search` / `gh api /search/code` command repeatedly without backoff
Still returns at least partial candidate results from the non-rate-limited backends — does not give up entirely
The query passed to skill-search references iOS simulators (e.g. 'ios simulator', 'ios sim', 'simulator')

evals.name: install-command-from-result-row
evals.prompt: I ran skill-search and the top result is `owner-mid/repo@chezmoi-helper` with 42 installs from skills.sh. Install it for me.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the `install_command` column from the skill-search result row rather than hand-crafting an install incantation. For an npx-skills result that means `npx skills add owner-mid/repo@chezmoi-helper` or equivalent.
expectations: Uses the install command surfaced by skill-search (the `install_command` column), not a hand-rolled `git clone` / manual file copy
For a `skills-cli` (a.k.a. `npx-skills`) result, the install command form is `npx skills add <owner>/<repo>@<skill>` (or equivalent shown in the row)
Reads the skill's `SKILL.md` before installing, OR explicitly notes it's relying on the user's prior review
Does NOT confuse the install command with the preview command (preview is for inspecting; install is for adding)

evals.name: sort-by-installs-when-popularity-matters
evals.prompt: Find me the most-installed React testing skills on skills.sh. I want to see what most people are actually using, not just what has the most stars on GitHub.
evals.expected_output: The agent passes `--sort-by installs` (with `--direction desc` implicit or explicit) so the npx-skills install counts drive the ordering, rather than GitHub stars.
expectations: Runs `skill-search search` with `--sort-by installs` (and not `--sort-by stars`)
Query references React and testing (e.g. 'react testing', 'react test', 'testing react')
Top results in the response are ordered by install count, not by stargazer count
Does NOT default to stars-based ranking when the user explicitly asked about install popularity

evals.name: raw-backend-for-debugging
evals.prompt: The Sourcegraph backend is returning weird results for my query — I want to see the raw Sourcegraph response so I can debug what file:(?i)skill\.md is matching.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses `skill-search raw sourcegraph <query>` to dump the backend-native JSON, rather than re-running the unified search or invoking `src search` directly.
expectations: Runs `skill-search raw sourcegraph` with the user's query
Does NOT invoke `src search` directly when the skill's `raw` subcommand is the documented escape hatch
Surfaces the raw JSON output (or its key fields like `Results`, `repository`, `file.path`) so the user can inspect what's matching

If the user asks about the query shape, references the documented `file:(?i)skill\.md <terms> select:file count:<limit>` form, where each token expands to `(file:<token> OR repo:<token> OR content:/(?m)^(name|description):.*<token>/)`

evals.name: authority-over-raw-signal-swiftui
evals.prompt: Find me an installable agent skill for working with iOS / SwiftUI. I want a recommendation.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs skill-search and sees both a large third-party collection (e.g. `dpearson2699/swift-ios-skills`) and a smaller-but-authoritative skill (e.g. `twostraws/swiftui-agent-skill@swiftui-pro` by Paul Hudson). It applies the workflow's authority check and recommends the authority-authored skill, citing source authority over raw stars / multi-source.
expectations: Runs `skill-search search` with a query referencing SwiftUI or iOS
After ranking results, explicitly considers source authority — names at least one recognized upstream authority for the SwiftUI domain (Apple, `twostraws` / Paul Hudson, `avanderlee`, or similar)
Does NOT default to recommending a larger third-party collection on raw star count or multi-source agreement alone when an authority-authored skill is present in the results

Recommends `twostraws/swiftui-agent-skill@swiftui-pro` OR an equivalent skill from a recognized SwiftUI authority (Apple, `avanderlee`, Hacking with Swift / `twostraws`), OR explicitly explains why no such skill is available in the result set
Uses the install command from the result row, not a hand-crafted incantation

evals.name: scope-fit-over-popularity-antislop
evals.prompt: Find me an installable agent skill for writing for humans — stripping AI slop and sounding natural. I want a recommendation.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs skill-search and sees both narrowly-scoped anti-slop skills (e.g. `third774/dotfiles@natural-writing`, `hardikpandya/stop-slop`) and broadly-scoped writing-quality skills (e.g. `obra/superpowers@writing-skills`). It applies the workflow's scope-fit check and recommends a narrowly-scoped option that targets AI-slop semantics, not a generic high-popularity writing skill that's merely adjacent.
expectations: Runs `skill-search search` with a query referencing slop, natural writing, anti-AI prose, or similar
After reading the top candidates' SKILL.md, explicitly contrasts scope — AI-slop / de-slopping is narrower than general writing quality / Strunk-style clarity / marketing copywriting

Recommends a skill whose SKILL.md is explicitly about AI tells, slop vocabulary, or natural-sounding prose (e.g. `third774/dotfiles@natural-writing`, `hardikpandya/stop-slop@stop-slop`, `brianlovin/claude-config@deslop`, or similar)
Does NOT recommend a generic high-install writing skill (e.g. `obra/superpowers@writing-skills`, `coreyhaines31/marketingskills@copywriting`) as the primary pick when a narrower match exists
Justifies the recommendation by what the skill actually targets, not by popularity alone

evals.name: niche-domain-low-install-fallback
evals.prompt: Find me an installable agent skill for working with chezmoi dotfiles. I want a recommendation.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs skill-search. Most chezmoi skills are below the 1K-install and 100-star bars. The agent does NOT refuse to recommend on those grounds — it applies the workflow's niche-domain guidance, falls back to the strongest multi-source-confirmed candidate with explicit caveats, or plainly states no candidate meets the bars and offers fallback help.
expectations: Runs `skill-search search` with a chezmoi-related query
Recognizes that chezmoi is a niche domain where the 1K-install / 100-star bars (calibrated for mainstream ecosystems) will exclude most or all candidates

Either recommends a multi-source-confirmed candidate (e.g. `mizchi/skills@chezmoi-management`, `terrylica/cc-skills@chezmoi-sync`, `majiayu000/claude-skill-registry@chezmoi`) with explicit caveats about install count, OR plainly states that no candidate meets the popularity bars and offers a fallback path (direct help, `npx skills init` to scaffold a local skill)
Does NOT refuse to recommend purely because the install-count threshold is unmet when multi-source agreement is present
Surfaces the multi-source-agreement signal as a partial substitute for low install counts when explaining the recommendation
